title: Synthetic IAM finding without trusted threat intel
description: Controlled Phase 3 P2 validation finding with no generic exploit wording.
what_is_wrong: Controlled Phase 3 P2 validation finding with no generic exploit wording.
what_the_fix_does: Guides removal of root user access keys and restoration of safer IAM access.

findings.title: Synthetic IAM finding without trusted threat intel

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_medium__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is medium and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.60, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.00, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is complete enough for advisory matrix placement. Policy override applied: this action is marked manual/high-risk and requires explicit exception-style review before execution.

execution_guidance.label: Disable root access key

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for arn:aws:iam::696505809372:access-key/AKIAOCYPHERISP2BASELINE in account 696505809372 (us-east-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Confirm break-glass and emergency automation does not depend on root access keys.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: Synthetic IAM finding without trusted threat intel After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended access changing scope for arn:aws:iam::696505809372:access-key/AKIAOCYPHERISP2BASELINE.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.
